title: Building and civil engineering technicians
description: Apply theory and principles of civil engineering in planning, designing, and overseeing construction and maintenance of structures and facilities under the direction of engineering staff or physical scientists. Perform surveying and mapping duties, usually under the direction of an engineer, surveyor, cartographer, or photogrammetrist, to obtain data used for construction, mapmaking, boundary location, mining, or other purposes. May calculate mapmaking information and create maps from source data, such as surveying notes, aerial photography, satellite data, or other maps to show topographical features, political boundaries, and other features. May verify accuracy and completeness of maps. Directly supervise and coordinate activities of workers engaged in firefighting and fire prevention and control. (Based on 11 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Inspect and monitor construction sites to ensure adherence to safety standards, building codes, or specifications.
Inspect newly installed energy-efficient equipment to ensure that it was installed properly and is performing according to specifications.
Conduct inspections using survey instruments, metering devices, and test equipment to examine construction quality and safety compliance.
Review and interpret plans, blueprints, site layouts, specifications, or construction methods to ensure compliance with legal requirements.
Measure dimensions and verify level, alignment, or elevation of structures or fixtures to ensure compliance with building plans.
Prepare reports and document project activities and data, including photographs, for compliance and quality assurance.
Supervise or coordinate activities of workers engaged in surveying, plotting data, and drafting maps or blueprints.
Calculate, measure, or mix refined feedstock used in mass construction projects and biofuel production.
Identify and compile database information to create requested maps and prepare topographic maps of surveyed land.
Perform calculations for mapping, including determining scales, angles, areas, and other relevant information.
Inspect bridges, roads, and other structures during and after construction for structural quality and safety.
Monitor installation of plumbing and electrical systems to ensure adherence to applicable regulations and codes.
Train, direct, or supervise other construction inspectors on compliance and quality assurance measures.
Conduct materials testing and analysis using engineering knowledge to assess compliance with standards.
Recommend changes to improve construction safety and compliance with building codes and regulations.
Educate clients about energy efficiency or provide information on energy-saving measures related to building projects.
Develop or review fire exit plans and participate in creating fire safety guidelines and evacuation schemes for nonresidential buildings.
Prepare and maintain reports of investigation results, and write detailed reports of fire inspections, including fire code violations and recommendations.
Schedule employee work assignments, set work priorities, and maintain records of employees' attendance and hours worked.
Direct investigation of suspected arson cases, hazards, and false alarms, and submit reports outlining findings.
Assess the nature and extent of fires and determine crew requirements and status of resources.
Analyze burn conditions, prepare postburn reports, and evaluate performance of assigned personnel.
Inspect and test existing fire protection systems and maintain required maps and records.
Evaluate standards, budgets, and production goals based on equipment and labor availability.
